sections.title: What prepositions do (core logic)
sections.content:
Prepositions link nouns to the rest of the sentence. They express **relationships** in time, place, movement, manner, or cause.

**Why it matters (roots-first)**: These tiny words are part of the Prepositions & Particles root (#6 in the Tree Model). They are rule-face logic (spatial/temporal relations) + lexical-face chunks. Wrong preposition = wrong meaning or unnatural English. English prepositions rarely translate word-for-word from Czech or other L1s — learn the distinctions, not lists.

**Key principle**: Start with the big systematic contrasts (at/on/in for time & place, movement pairs). Then add fixed uses.

sections.title: Time: at / on / in
sections.content:
**The big three for time** — one of the highest-impact distinctions in English.

**Why it matters**: Scheduling, stories, and exams all turn on this. "Meet at 5" vs "on Monday" vs "in July" tells the listener exactly when — or confuses them completely.

**Contrast examples**:
- I wake up **at** 7 o'clock. (exact clock time / night / weekend)
- The lesson is **on** Monday 15 March. (specific day or date)
- She was born **in** 2020 / **in** the morning / **in** summer. (longer periods, months, years, parts of day)

**Rule of thumb**: at = point in time; on = day surface; in = container/period.

diagram.title: at / on / in for time
rows: at 5 o'clock, at noon, at night, at the weekend
on Monday, on 15th March, on my birthday
in August, in 2025, in the morning, in winter

sections.title: Place: at / on / in
sections.content:
**The big three for place** — the same three prepositions, completely different logic from time.

**Why it matters**: Location errors are among the most common and noticeable. "The keys are in the car" (inside) is not the same as "on the car" (surface).

**Contrast examples**:
- The book is **in** the box / **in** London / **in** the room. (enclosed space or area)
- The picture is **on** the wall / **on** the table. (surface or line)
- Meet me **at** the door / **at** the bus stop / **at** home. (specific point or location)

**Cities & countries**: usually **in**. Specific points/addresses: often **at**.

diagram.title: at / on / in for place
rows: in the box, in London, in the room
on the table, on the wall, on the street
At a specific point or place
at the door, at the bus stop, at home

sections.title: Movement and direction
sections.content:
Movement prepositions describe **change of position** (vs static place).

**Core families & contrasts**:
- **to / into** = direction toward or inside a goal (go to school, get into the car)
- **from / out of** = origin or leaving (from London, get out of the car)
- **through** = crossing an enclosed space side-to-side (through the tunnel / park)
- **across** = crossing a surface side-to-side (across the road / bridge)
- **along** = following the length of something (along the river)

**Why it matters**: "I walked **to** the park" (I arrived) vs "I walked **in** the park" (I was already inside). Direction vs location is fundamental.

**Practice contrast**: across the bridge (surface) vs through the forest (inside area).

sections.title: Duration, deadlines & periods
sections.content:
Time expressions that answer "How long?" or "By when?"

**Key contrasts**:
- **for** + duration (how long something lasts): for two hours, for a week
- **since** + starting point (still continuing): since 2020, since Monday
- **by** = no later than (deadline): by Friday, by 5 pm
- **until** = continues up to that point: until midnight, wait until tomorrow
- **during** = at some point within a known period: during the meeting, during the film

**Why it matters**: "I've studied English **for** six months" (length) tells a different story from "**since** January" (when it began). These are high-frequency in real life and gap-fills.

**Bonus pair**: **before** / **after** (earlier/later than a point).

sections.title: Common errors & traps
sections.content:
**High-frequency mistakes** (often L1 interference):

- interested **on** music → **interested in**
- It depends **from** you → **depends on**
- She arrived **to** Prague → **arrived in** Prague (cities) / **at** the station (points)
- I go **to** home every day → **I go home** (no preposition with home, school, work in this sense)
- good **in** maths → **good at** maths

**Why it matters**: These appear constantly in speaking, writing, and every exam gap-fill / open cloze. Fixing them gives immediate visible progress.

Many more are fixed "dependent" combinations — see the separate Dependent Prepositions topic.

sections.title: Other high-frequency uses + roadmap
sections.content:
**Useful everyday prepositions** (beyond the big groups):

- **by** = means of transport or author (by train, written by...)
- **with / without** = together or using / lacking (with a friend, cut with a knife, without help)
- **of** = belonging, material, or part (the capital of France, made of wood, cup of tea)
- **as** = role or function (work as a teacher, use as a tool)
- **like** = similarity (looks like, sounds like)

**Why it matters**: These are in almost every paragraph you read or write.

**Direct practice in this curriculum** (start here after the cards):
- Guided MC (core at/on/in + time/place)
- Gap fill – Simple (time & place basics)
- Gap fill – Advanced & Tricky (movement, deadlines, idioms)
- Gap fill – Top 40 (all common prepositions in context)

**Next steps**: [[topic:prepositions_dependent|Dependent Prepositions]] (B2 fixed verb/noun/adjective + prep chunks — critical for exams). Full reference list: [[preplist|Prepositions list]].

questions.question: 'At 5 o'clock' – what does 'at' express here?
questions.explanation: 'At' with a clock time is Time (point) – a point in time. Some times are periods, e.g. in 1980. Place (point) is for location, e.g. at the door.

questions.question: 'The picture is on the wall.' – What does 'on' express?
questions.explanation: 'On' with a surface (wall) is place – something on a line or surface.

questions.question: Which preposition often goes with enclosed spaces (room, box, city)?
questions.explanation: 'In' is used for enclosed spaces: in the room, in the box, in London.

questions.question: The keys are ____ the box. (We keep them inside.)
questions.explanation: Inside a space → in.

questions.question: The picture is ____ the wall.
questions.explanation: On a surface → on.

questions.question: We'll meet ____ the bus stop.
questions.explanation: At a point or place → at.

questions.question: I get up ____ 7 o'clock every day.
questions.explanation: Use **at** with exact clock times (at 7 o'clock, at noon).

questions.question: The meeting is ____ Monday.
questions.explanation: Use **on** with days and dates (on Monday, on 15th March).

questions.question: She was born ____ 1995.
questions.explanation: Use **in** with years, months and seasons (in 1995, in January).

questions.question: The book is lying ____ the table. (on the surface)
questions.explanation: **On** is used when something is on top of a surface.

questions.question: He lives ____ London.
questions.explanation: We live **in** a city or country (in London, in Spain).

questions.question: We'll meet ____ the bus stop.
questions.explanation: Use **at** for a specific point or meeting place (at the bus stop).

questions.question: I'm going ____ the supermarket.
questions.explanation: Use **to** for movement towards a place (to the supermarket).

questions.question: She's been waiting ____ two hours.
questions.explanation: **For** + period of time (for two hours).

questions.question: I've lived here ____ 2020.
questions.explanation: **Since** + starting point in time (since 2020).

questions.question: I'll see you ____ the morning.
questions.explanation: Parts of the day usually take **in** (in the morning), except at night.

gapfill_simple.title: Practice: Gap fill – Simple (time and place)

questions.question: I get up ____ 7 o'clock every day.
questions.explanation: We use **at** with exact clock times (at 7 o'clock, at noon, at midnight). Use **on** for days (on Monday) and **in** for longer periods (in 1995, in summer).

questions.question: The meeting is ____ Monday.
questions.explanation: We use **on** with days of the week and dates (on Monday, on 5th March). Use **at** for clock times and **in** for months, years or seasons.

questions.question: She was born ____ 1995.
questions.explanation: We use **in** with years, months and seasons (in 1995, in January, in summer). Use **on** for specific days and **at** for clock times.

questions.question: The book is lying ____ the table. (on the surface)
questions.explanation: We use **on** when something is on top of a surface (on the table, on the wall). 'Lying' implies contact with the surface → **on**.

questions.question: He lives ____ London.
questions.explanation: We use **in** with cities, countries and enclosed or large areas (in London, in the box, in the room). Use **at** for a specific point (at the station) and **on** for a street or surface.

questions.question: We'll meet ____ the bus stop.
questions.explanation: We use **at** for a specific point or place where something happens (at the bus stop, at the door, at home). Use **in** for being inside a building or area.

questions.question: I'm going ____ the supermarket.
questions.explanation: We use **to** for movement or direction towards a place (to the supermarket, to school, to work). Use **at** when we are already there (at the supermarket).

questions.question: She's been waiting ____ two hours.
questions.explanation: We use **for** to say how long something lasts (for two hours, for a week). Use **since** when we say when it started (since 9 o'clock) and **during** when we say when it happened (during the meeting).

questions.question: I've lived here ____ 2020.
questions.explanation: We use **since** with a point in time when something started and is still true (since 2020, since Monday). Use **for** with a duration (for three years).

questions.question: I'll see you ____ the morning.
questions.explanation: We use **in** with parts of the day (in the morning, in the afternoon) but **at** for night (at night).

questions.question: The shop opens ____ 9 am.
questions.explanation: Exact time → at (at 9 am, at noon).

questions.question: She was born ____ 15th March.
questions.explanation: Specific date → on (on 15th March, on Monday).

questions.question: We're going on holiday ____ August.
questions.explanation: Month → in (in August, in 2024).

questions.question: The pen is lying ____ the desk. (on the surface)
questions.explanation: On top of a surface → **on**. 'Lying' implies contact with the surface.

questions.question: They live ____ the end of the street.
questions.explanation: A specific point (end of the street) → at.

questions.question: I've been learning English ____ six months.
questions.explanation: Duration (how long) → for.

questions.question: We stayed ____ midnight.
questions.explanation: Up to a time (we stayed up to midnight) → until.

questions.question: The answer is ____ the bottom of the page.
questions.explanation: A specific point (bottom of the page) → at.

questions.question: The project must be finished ____ Friday.
questions.explanation: We use **by** to mean 'no later than' or 'before' a time (by Friday, by 5 pm). Use **until** when something continues up to that time (wait until Friday), and **on** for the day itself (on Friday).

questions.question: We walked right ____ the park from one side to the other to reach the station.
questions.explanation: We use **through** when we move from one side of an area or space to the other (through the park, through the tunnel). Use **across** for a surface (across the road) and **along** for following a line (along the path).

questions.question: She sat right ____ me in the cinema so we could share the popcorn.
questions.explanation: We use **beside** or **next to** when one thing is at the side of another (beside me, next to the window). Use **between** for something in the middle of two others (between you and me).

questions.question: Divide the sweets ____ the four children.
questions.explanation: We use **between** when we talk about two or more separate people or things (between the four children, between A and B). Use **among** for a general group when we don't name them individually (among the crowd).

questions.question: I'll be there ____ 9 and 10 o'clock.
questions.explanation: We use **between** for a time (or place) in the middle of two points (between 9 and 10, between Monday and Friday). Use **from...to** for a range (from 9 to 10).

questions.question: We discussed the problem ____ the meeting.
questions.explanation: We use **during** when something happens within a period or event (during the meeting, during the film). Use **for** for how long (for two hours) and **while** with a clause (while we were meeting).

questions.question: The cat is hiding ____ the sofa—you can see its tail sticking out from underneath.
questions.explanation: We use **under** when something is below or beneath another thing (under the sofa, under the table). Use **below** for a lower level and **over** for above.

questions.question: They walked ____ the bridge.
questions.explanation: We use **across** when we go from one side of a surface or area to the other (across the bridge, across the road). Use **through** for going inside something (through the tunnel) and **along** for following the length (along the bridge).

questions.question: We drove ____ the coast for miles, following the shoreline.
questions.explanation: We use **along** when we follow a line or length (along the coast, along the river, along the road). Use **through** for from one end to the other inside something, and **across** for from one side to the other.

questions.question: He arrived ____ train.
questions.explanation: We use **by** with means of transport when we say how we travel (by train, by car, by plane). Use **on** for some transport (on foot, on the bus) and **in** for others (in a car, in a taxi).

questions.question: The ball rolled ____ the table and onto the floor.
questions.explanation: Off = from on top of something down or away (off the table).

questions.question: There's a poster ____ the wall above the sofa.
questions.explanation: On a vertical surface (the wall) → on.

questions.question: We cycled ____ the river for about five miles.
questions.explanation: Following a line (the river) → along.

questions.question: The tunnel goes ____ the mountain.
questions.explanation: From one side to the other inside something (the mountain) → through.

questions.question: She stood ____ the window, looking out.
questions.explanation: At the side of → by, beside, or next to.

questions.question: The deadline is ____ the end of the week.
questions.explanation: No later than → by.

questions.question: We had a break ____ the journey.
questions.explanation: Within the time of the journey → during.

questions.question: The temperature dropped ____ zero.
questions.explanation: Lower than (temperature) → below.

questions.question: They ran ____ the road to the other side.
questions.explanation: From one side of a surface to the other → across.

gapfill_tricky.title: Practice: Gap fill – Tricky and idiomatic

questions.question: I'm interested ____ learning Spanish.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). The fixed phrase is **interested in** (interested in music, interested in learning). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's good ____ maths.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). We say **good at** a subject or skill (good at maths, good at swimming). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: We're looking ____ our keys.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). **Look for** means to search for or try to find (looking for our keys, look for a job). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: It depends ____ the weather.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). The fixed phrase is **depend on** (it depends on the weather, it depends on you). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: He's afraid ____ spiders.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). We say **afraid of** (afraid of spiders, afraid of the dark). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: I'm tired ____ waiting.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). We say **tired of** when we've had enough of something (tired of waiting, tired of this weather). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She insisted ____ paying the bill.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). The fixed phrase is **insist on** (insist on paying, insist on coming). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: They complained ____ the noise.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). We say **complain about** something (complain about the noise, complain about the service). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: We need to focus ____ the main point.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). We say **focus on** something (focus on the main point, focus on your work). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: The book was written ____ a famous author.
questions.explanation: Passive agent: **by** introduces who or what does the action (written by a famous author). Not a dependent preposition.

questions.question: She's responsible ____ the project.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Responsible **for** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: He's very proud ____ his daughter.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Proud **of** someone or something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: We're worried ____ the results.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Worried **about** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's capable ____ doing the job.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Capable **of** doing something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: They're keen ____ starting soon.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Keen **on** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: I'm grateful ____ your help.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Grateful **for** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: He's allergic ____ nuts.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Allergic **to** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: We're familiar ____ the system.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Familiar **with** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's famous ____ her novels.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Famous **for** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: I need a cup ____ tea.
questions.explanation: **of** – part of / belonging (cup of tea, capital of France).

questions.question: She's proud ____ her son.
questions.explanation: **of** – after adjectives (proud of, afraid of, tired of).

questions.question: The table is made ____ wood.
questions.explanation: **of** – material (made of wood).

questions.question: I'm going ____ the station.
questions.explanation: **to** – direction (go to, walk to).

questions.question: Give it ____ me.
questions.explanation: **to** – recipient (give to, say to).

questions.question: He's allergic ____ nuts.
questions.explanation: **to** – fixed phrase (allergic to).

questions.question: She was born ____ 1995.
questions.explanation: **in** – year/month (in 1995, in August).

questions.question: I'm interested ____ history.
questions.explanation: **in** – interested in (fixed phrase).

questions.question: She's been waiting ____ two hours.
questions.explanation: **for** – duration (for two hours).

questions.question: This present is ____ you.
questions.explanation: **for** – intended for (for you, for the meeting).

questions.question: What's the reason ____ the delay?
questions.explanation: **for** – reason for (fixed phrase).

questions.question: The book is lying ____ the table. (on the surface)
questions.explanation: **on** – on a surface (on the table). 'Lying' implies contact with the surface.

questions.question: We meet ____ Monday.
questions.explanation: **on** – day (on Monday, on 5th March).

questions.question: It depends ____ the weather.
questions.explanation: **on** – depend on (fixed phrase).

questions.question: I went ____ my friend.
questions.explanation: **with** – together with (with a friend).

questions.question: Cut it ____ a knife.
questions.explanation: **with** – means (with a knife, with a pen).

questions.question: We'll meet ____ the bus stop.
questions.explanation: **at** – point or place (at the bus stop, at home).

questions.question: The shop opens ____ 9 am.
questions.explanation: **at** – clock time (at 9 am, at noon).

questions.question: She's good ____ maths.
questions.explanation: **at** – good at (fixed phrase).

questions.question: He arrived ____ train.
questions.explanation: **by** – means of transport (by train, by car).

questions.question: Finish it ____ Friday.
questions.explanation: **by** – no later than (by Friday).

questions.question: The book was written ____ a famous author.
questions.explanation: **by** – agent in passive (written by).

questions.question: The parcel came ____ Paris.
questions.explanation: **from** – origin (the parcel came from Paris = it was sent from there).

questions.question: I've lived here ____ 2020.
questions.explanation: **since** – from (time) until now (since 2020).

questions.question: He suffers ____ headaches.
questions.explanation: **from** – suffer from (fixed phrase).

questions.question: We talked ____ the project.
questions.explanation: **about** – concerning (talk about, about the project).

questions.question: She's worried ____ the exam.
questions.explanation: **about** – worried about (fixed phrase).

questions.question: What's the film ____?
questions.explanation: **about** – concerning (a film about war).

questions.question: He works ____ a teacher.
questions.explanation: **as** – role (work as, act as).

questions.question: She went ____ the room.
questions.explanation: **into** – movement inside (into the room).

questions.question: That sounds ____ a good idea.
questions.explanation: **like** – similar to (sound like, look like).

questions.question: We walked ____ the park.
questions.explanation: **through** – from one side to the other (through the park, through the tunnel).

questions.question: The bridge goes ____ the river.
questions.explanation: **over** – above, across (over the river, over the bridge).

questions.question: We'll start ____ dinner.
questions.explanation: **after** – later than (after dinner, after 5 pm).

questions.question: I'll be there ____ 9 and 10.
questions.explanation: **between** – in the middle of two (between 9 and 10).

questions.question: Get ____ the car quickly.
questions.explanation: **out of** – exit (get out of, out of the room).

questions.question: The cat is ____ the sofa.
questions.explanation: **under** – below (under the sofa, under the table).

questions.question: Please finish ____ 5 pm.
questions.explanation: **before** – earlier than (before 5 pm).

questions.question: We have no money. We can't buy it ____ money. (one word: not having)
questions.explanation: **without** – not having (we can't buy it without money).

questions.question: It costs ____ 10 pounds.
questions.explanation: **about** – approximately (about 10).

questions.question: They sat ____ the table.
questions.explanation: **around** – surrounding (around the table).

questions.question: I've known her ____ 2020.
questions.explanation: **since** – from (time) until now.

questions.question: That's ____ the law.
questions.explanation: **against** – opposing (against the law).

questions.question: We met ____ the meeting.
questions.explanation: **during** – within a period (during the meeting).

questions.question: We stayed ____ midnight.
questions.explanation: **until** – up to a time (until midnight).

questions.question: We discussed it ____ ourselves.
questions.explanation: **among** – in a group (among friends).

questions.question: They ran ____ the road.
questions.explanation: **across** – from one side to the other (across the road).

questions.question: The keys are ____ the door. (We hid them at the back so nobody would see them.)
questions.explanation: **behind** – at the back of (behind the door). Context (hid at the back) rules out by, on, in.

questions.question: The plane flew ____ the clouds.
questions.explanation: **above** – higher than (above the clouds).

questions.question: The temperature dropped ____ zero.
questions.explanation: **below** – lower than (below zero).

questions.question: They live ____ the station.
questions.explanation: **near** – close to (near the station).

questions.question: We walked ____ the riverbank for a couple of miles.
questions.explanation: **along** – following a line (along the riverbank).

questions.question: The ball rolled ____ the table.
questions.explanation: **off** – away from a surface (off the table).

questions.question: She ran ____ the stairs.
questions.explanation: **up** – direction (up the stairs).

questions.question: We walked ____ the hill.
questions.explanation: **down** – direction (down the hill).

questions.question: They drove ____ the city.
questions.explanation: **towards** – in the direction of (towards the city).

questions.question: We'll finish ____ an hour.
questions.explanation: **within** – inside a time or space (within an hour).

questions.question: Once ____ a time there was a king.
questions.explanation: **upon** – formal for **on** (once upon a time).

questions.question: Everyone came ____ Tom.
questions.explanation: **except** – not including (everyone except Tom).

questions.question: They accused him ____ stealing.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Accuse someone **of** something (accuse of stealing, accuse of lying). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: She suffers ____ migraines.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Suffer **from** an illness or problem (suffer from headaches). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: He apologized ____ being late.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Apologize **for** something (apologize for the delay). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: I don't approve ____ that plan.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Approve **of** something (approve of the idea). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: She applied ____ the job.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Apply **for** a job or place (apply for a visa). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: We arrived ____ the station at 8.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Arrive **at** a place (arrive at the airport). Use **in** for cities/countries. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: He believes ____ climate change.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Believe **in** something (believe in justice). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: They complained ____ the manager about the noise.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Complain **to** someone (complain to the manager). Complain **about** something. See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: She congratulated him ____ passing the exam.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Congratulate someone **on** something (congratulate on your success). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: We consist ____ ten members.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Consist **of** = be made up of (the team consists of five people). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: He decided ____ a new car.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Decide **on** something (decide on a date). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: She dreams ____ travelling the world.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Dream **of** something (dream of success). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: They insisted ____ paying for dinner.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Insist **on** doing something (insist on coming). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: He listened ____ the radio.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Listen **to** something (listen to music). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: She objected ____ the proposal.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb). Object **to** something (object to the plan). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Verbs.

questions.question: She's famous ____ her research.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Famous **for** something (famous for her work). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: He's aware ____ the problem.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Aware **of** something (aware of the risk). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: I'm bad ____ remembering names.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Bad **at** something (bad at maths). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's capable ____ making quick decisions.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Capable **of** doing something (capable of winning). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: They're excited ____ the trip.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Excited **about** something (excited about the holiday). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: He's good ____ languages.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Good **at** a skill or subject (good at swimming). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: We're grateful ____ your support.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Grateful **for** something (grateful for your help). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's interested ____ archaeology.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Interested **in** something (interested in history). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: He's jealous ____ his brother's success.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Jealous **of** someone or something (jealous of her). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: I'm keen ____ learning to cook.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Keen **on** something (keen on sport). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: They're nervous ____ the interview.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Nervous **about** something (nervous about the exam). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's proud ____ her team.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Proud **of** someone or something (proud of his son). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: He's responsible ____ the budget.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Responsible **for** something (responsible for the project). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: We're tired ____ waiting.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Tired **of** something (tired of this weather). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's worried ____ the delay.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective). Worried **about** something (worried about him). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Adjectives.

questions.question: She's the leader ____ the team.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Leader **of** something (leader of the party). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: There's been an increase ____ demand.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Increase **in** something (increase in prices). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: He has a lot of experience ____ teaching.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Experience **in** something (experience in teaching). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: What's the reason ____ the delay?
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Reason **for** something (reason for the change). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: She has a good relationship ____ her boss.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Relationship **with** someone (relationship with his parents). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: There's a solution ____ the problem.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Solution **to** a problem (answer to the question). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: He made a complaint ____ the service.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Complaint **about** something (complaint about the noise). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: She has a fear ____ flying.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Fear **of** something (fear of heights). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: There's a demand ____ better transport.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Demand **for** something (demand for action). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: He showed an interest ____ the project.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Interest **in** something (interest in art). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: She gave a talk ____ climate change.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Talk **on** something (a talk on health). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: There's a difference ____ the two versions.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Difference **between** two things (difference between A and B). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: He has a talent ____ music.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Talent **for** something (talent for languages). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: She has confidence ____ her ability.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Confidence **in** someone or something (confidence in the team). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: There's a need ____ more staff.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun). Need **for** something (need for change). See Reference → Dependent prepositions: Nouns.

questions.question: 'At 5 o'clock' – what does 'at' express here?
questions.explanation: 'At' with a clock time is Time (point) – a point in time. Some times are periods, e.g. in 1980. Place (point) is for location, e.g. at the door.

questions.question: 'The picture is on the wall.' – What does 'on' express?
questions.explanation: 'On' with a surface (wall) is place – something on a line or surface.

questions.question: Which preposition often goes with enclosed spaces (room, box, city)?
questions.explanation: 'In' is used for enclosed spaces: in the room, in the box, in London.

questions.question: The keys are ____ the box. (We keep them inside.)
questions.explanation: Inside a space → in.

questions.question: The picture is ____ the wall.
questions.explanation: On a surface → on.

questions.question: We'll meet ____ the bus stop.
questions.explanation: At a point or place → at.

questions.question: 'At 5 o'clock' – what does 'at' express here?
questions.explanation: 'At' with a clock time is Time (point) – a point in time. Some times are periods, e.g. in 1980. Place (point) is for location, e.g. at the door.

questions.question: 'The picture is on the wall.' – What does 'on' express?
questions.explanation: 'On' with a surface (wall) is place – something on a line or surface.

questions.question: Which preposition often goes with enclosed spaces (room, box, city)?
questions.explanation: 'In' is used for enclosed spaces: in the room, in the box, in London.

questions.question: The keys are ____ the box. (We keep them inside.)
questions.explanation: Inside a space → in.
